message:
fix(core,cli): evaluate column DEFAULT expressions in INSERT and handle REPL interrupts

Connection / INSERT handling (connection.rs):
- INSERT statements now evaluate column DEFAULT expressions (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,
  literal values, etc.) for columns not explicitly listed in the INSERT column
  list, instead of filling them with NULL. This affects INSERT ... VALUES,
  INSERT ... SELECT, INSERT ... DEFAULT VALUES, and trigger snapshot rows.
- New collect_insert_default_row() builds a pre-populated row vector with each
  column's default value (or NULL when no DEFAULT is declared), which
  map_insert_source_row_to_table_row then overlays with supplied values.
- Includes handling for CURRENT_TIMESTAMP / CURRENT_DATE / CURRENT_TIME
  defaults using frozen Unix epoch time, and literal integer/real/text/blob
  defaults.

CLI REPL (main.rs):
- Handle ErrorKind::Interrupted on read_line by clearing pending SQL and
  continuing the REPL loop, so Ctrl-C doesn't kill the shell but instead
  cancels the current multi-line input.
- Add InterruptOnceBufRead test helper and test verifying the shell survives
  one interrupt and processes subsequent input normally.

Minor adjustments in datetime, MVCC lifecycle, E2E, and VFS unix crates
to align with the new default-value evaluation paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
